200.q: Who is Mary Jane Watson?

200.q: Who is Gwen Stacy?
200.a: Peter's first love — her death is one of the most impactful moments in comics history

200.q: Who is Aunt May?
200.a: Peter's guardian who raised him after his parents' death — she doesn't know his secret for years

200.q: Who is the Green Goblin?
200.a: Norman Osborn — a scientist who becomes unhinged after taking an experimental formula

200.q: What studio produces Spider-Man films?
200.a: Sony owns the rights, but Marvel Studios co-produced the MCU Spider-Man films with Tom Holland

200.q: What is the Daily Bugle?
200.a: The newspaper where Peter works as a photographer — run by J. Jonah Jameson

400.q: Who is Doctor Octopus?
400.a: Otto Octavius — a scientist with mechanical tentacle arms who is one of Spider-Man's greatest foes

400.q: What is the Sinister Six?
400.a: A team of six of Spider-Man's deadliest villains working together to destroy him

400.q: What is the black symbiote suit?
400.a: An alien symbiote that enhances Peter's powers but increases his aggression — it bonds with Eddie Brock to become Venom

400.q: Who is Venom?
400.a: Eddie Brock bonded with the symbiote — one of Spider-Man's most complex enemies

400.q: Who is Mysterio in the MCU?
400.a: Quentin Beck — a former Stark employee who creates illusions. He nearly exposes Peter's identity

400.q: What is the significance of No Way Home?
400.a: All three live-action Spider-Men unite — Tobey Maguire, Andrew Garfield, and Tom Holland

400.q: Who is the Prowler?
400.a: Aaron Davis — Miles Morales's uncle and a villain in the Spider-Verse

400.q: Who is Silk?
400.a: Cindy Moon — bitten by the same spider as Peter, she has silk-spinning abilities

400.q: What is the Spider-Verse?
400.a: The multiverse concept centred on Spider-People — all versions of Spider-Man across parallel dimensions

600.a: A 1990s comics arc where Peter discovers he might be a clone of himself — a controversial but significant storyline

600.q: What is the One More Day controversy?
600.a: Peter makes a deal with Mephisto erasing his marriage to MJ — widely considered the worst Spider-Man story ever told

600.q: What is Superior Spider-Man?
600.a: Otto Octavius takes over Peter's body — he becomes Spider-Man with a superior but villainous approach

600.q: What is the significance of Gwen Stacy's death?
600.a: Often considered the end of the Silver Age of comics — her neck was snapped by the sudden stop of the web catching her

600.q: What is the Kingpin's connection to Spider-Man?
600.a: Wilson Fisk controls much of New York's crime — his schemes frequently put Peter's loved ones at risk

600.q: Who is the Spider-Man of 2099?
600.a: Miguel O'Hara — a futuristic Spider-Man in a dystopian New York who appears in the Spider-Verse films

600.q: What is Kaine Parker?
600.a: A degenerated clone of Peter with a touch of death ability — later becomes the Scarlet Spider

600.q: What is the significance of the Symkarian Spider-Man (Silver Sable)?
600.a: She represents how Spider-Man's world intersects with international politics and mercenaries

600.q: What is the Great Web?
600.a: In Spider-Verse lore — the metaphysical structure connecting all Spider-Totems across dimensions

600.q: What is the deeper theme of Spider-Man?
600.a: Responsibility, sacrifice, and the cost of power — Peter perpetually sacrifices personal happiness for the greater good
